test_cases.prompt:
You are running bug-investigation. A user has reported the following bug. Investigate it and produce a BUGS.md entry with your findings.

## Bug Report
{fixture:1-clear-reproduction-steps}

## Project Context
This is a Python/Flask web application with PostgreSQL. The project is at /projects/webapp. The memory directory is /projects/webapp/hack. BUGS.md should be written to /projects/webapp/hack/BUGS.md.
expected_behaviors: Traces from symptom (login failure) to root cause (string interpolation in SQL query at src/auth/password_check.py:18)
Produces BUGS.md entry with Status, Impact, Root Cause, Files Involved with line references
Includes Resolution Plan with actionable steps such as switching to parameterized query

test_cases.prompt:
You are running bug-investigation. A user has reported a vague performance issue. Investigate it and produce a BUGS.md entry with your findings.

## Bug Report
{fixture:2-vague-bug-report}

## Project Context
This is a Python/Flask web application with PostgreSQL, deployed on AWS ECS. The project is at /projects/webapp. The memory directory is /projects/webapp/hack. BUGS.md should be written to /projects/webapp/hack/BUGS.md.
expected_behaviors: Does NOT jump to a single cause without investigation — examines all potential hotspots
Examines the N+1 query pattern, the unbuffered file read, and the synchronous HTTP call
Identifies synchronous HTTP call to weather API as most likely cause based on evidence (15s timeout, external dependency, intermittent latency matches user report)
Produces structured BUGS.md entry with Investigating or Root Cause Found status

test_cases.prompt:
You are running bug-investigation. The QA team has reported three bugs simultaneously after a staging deployment. Handle all three and produce BUGS.md entries for each.

## Bug Reports
{fixture:3-multi-bug-batch}

## Project Context
This is a Python/Flask web application with PostgreSQL, deployed to staging. The project is at /projects/webapp. The memory directory is /projects/webapp/hack. BUGS.md should be written to /projects/webapp/hack/BUGS.md. Use sequential IDs starting from BUG-001.
expected_behaviors: Handles all 3 bugs as separate BUG entries with independent investigation
Dispatches or documents separate investigation per bug rather than combining them
Produces 3 distinct BUGS.md entries with independent Status, Impact, and Resolution Plans
